## Gallery Page Context — Full Report The gallery is implemented entirely with a **custom-built lightbox and scroll-reveal** (no third-party lightbox/animation libraries). Images are **hardcoded** in the controller — there is **no database table, model, or migration** for gallery images. --- ### 1. Controller method — `app/Http/Controllers/PageController.php` The `gallery()` method hardcodes an array of 6 images and passes them to the view via `compact('images')`: ```php public function gallery() { $images = [ ['src' => 'images/classroom.jpg', 'alt' => 'Students studying in classroom'], ['src' => 'images/students-girls.jpg', 'alt' => 'Female students in uniform'], ['src' => 'images/students-group.jpg', 'alt' => 'Students with their teacher'], ['src' => 'images/islamic-architecture.jpg', 'alt' => 'Islamic architecture and mosque'], ['src' => 'images/admissions-flyer.jpg', 'alt' => 'Admissions flyer'], ['src' => 'images/admissions-student.jpg', 'alt' => 'Student during admission'], ]; return view('pages.gallery', compact('images')); } ``` Note: `PageController` also has `home()`, `about()`, `admissions()`, `academics()`. Other controllers: `AdminController`, `ContactController`, base `Controller`. ### 2. Route — `routes/web.php` ```php Route::get('/gallery', [PageController::class, 'gallery'])->name('gallery'); ``` The navbar (`resources/views/components/navbar.blade.php` L7) links to it: `['route' => 'gallery', 'label' => 'Gallery']`. The home page's "Gallery Preview" section also links via `route('gallery')`. ### 3. Layout — `resources/views/layouts/app.blade.php` - Asset includes via Vite: `@vite(['resources/css/app.css', 'resources/js/app.js'])` - Google Fonts: Amiri (Arabic) + Plus Jakarta Sans - **No external lightbox library is loaded** (no GLightbox/PhotoSwipe/Litebox/jQuery — none in package.json either) - A **custom lightbox overlay is hardcoded in the layout** (global, not page-specific): ```html

@yield('scripts') ``` The layout uses `` and `` Blade components and yields `content`, `title`, and `scripts`. ### 4. JavaScript — `resources/js/` Only two files exist: `app.js` and `bootstrap.js` (bootstrap.js just sets up axios). **No separate gallery/lightbox JS file.** All gallery-related JS is inline in `resources/js/app.js` inside a single `DOMContentLoaded` handler: - **Mobile menu toggle** (`#nav-toggle` / `#nav-menu`) - **Scroll reveal** via `IntersectionObserver`: selects all `.reveal` elements, adds `.revealed` class when intersecting (threshold 0.15, rootMargin `0px 0px -50px 0px`), then unobserves. - **Custom lightbox**: selects `[data-lightbox]` links; on click reads `data-lightbox` (src) + `data-alt` (alt), sets `#lightbox-img`, adds `.active` to `#lightbox`, hides body scroll. Closes on `#lightbox-close` click, overlay click, or `Escape` key. - **Navbar shadow on scroll** (`#navbar`). ### 5. CSS/SCSS — `resources/css/app.css` Only one CSS file (no SCSS/SASS exists). Tailwind v4 (`@import 'tailwindcss';`) with `@source` directives. Custom theme: primary green palette + gold, fonts, and keyframe animations (`fade-in`, `slide-up`, `fade-in-up`). Gallery/lightbox-relevant custom CSS: ```css /* Scroll reveal */ .reveal { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(40px); transition: opacity 0.7s ease-out, transform 0.7s ease-out; } .reveal.revealed { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); } /* Lightbox */ .lightbox-overlay { position: fixed; inset: 0; z-index: 100; display: flex; ... opacity: 0; pointer-events: none; transition: opacity 0.3s ease; } .lightbox-overlay.active { opacity: 1; pointer-events: auto; } .lightbox-overlay img { max-width: 90vw; max-height: 85vh; ... transform: scale(0.9); transition: transform 0.3s ease; } .lightbox-overlay.active img { transform: scale(1); } .lightbox-close { ... } .lightbox-close:hover { color: #86efac; } ``` ### 6. package.json dependencies ```json "devDependencies": { "@tailwindcss/vite": "^4.0.0", "axios": "^1.11.0", "concurrently": "^9.0.1", "laravel-vite-plugin": "^2.0.0", "tailwindcss": "^4.0.0", "vite": "^7.0.7" } ``` No `dependencies` block. **No lightbox, jQuery, AOS, GSAP, or any animation/image library** — everything is custom. Build stack: Vite 7 + Tailwind v4 via `@tailwindcss/vite`. `vite.config.js` inputs `resources/css/app.css` and `resources/js/app.js`. ### 7. Config / database migrations for gallery images **None exist.** Migrations directory contains only: `create_users_table`, `create_cache_table`, `create_jobs_table`, and `2026_06_30_000000_create_contacts_table`. Models: only `Contact` and `User` — **no `Image` or `Gallery` model**. Config files are all standard Laravel defaults (no gallery config). Gallery images are entirely hardcoded (in the controller for the gallery page, and separately re-hardcoded in `home.blade.php` L369-375 for the homepage preview — note the two lists are not shared/synced). --- ### Cross-codebase search results - **"gallery"** → 11 files: `PageController.php`, `web.php`, `gallery.blade.php`, `home.blade.php`, `navbar.blade.php`, `footer.blade.php`, `PROJECT_DOCUMENTATION.md` (+ compiled view cache files). - **"lightbox"** → 5 files: `PROJECT_DOCUMENTATION.md`, `app.css`, `app.js`, `layouts/app.blade.php`, `pages/gallery.blade.php`. - **"reveal"** → 9 files: `app.css`, `app.js`, `section-header.blade.php`, and pages `about/academics/admissions/contact/gallery/home.blade.php` (the `.reveal` class is used site-wide, not gallery-specific). ### The gallery view itself (`gallery.blade.php`) Extends `layouts.app`, title "Gallery | Abu Aneefah Islamic Academy". Renders a **masonry-style grid** (`columns-1 sm:columns-2 lg:columns-3` with `break-inside-avoid`) looping `$images`. Each item is an `` with `data-lightbox="{{ asset($image['src']) }}"` and `data-alt`, plus the `.reveal` class for scroll animation, hover zoom (`group-hover:scale-110`), and an overlay "eye" icon + caption. Includes an empty-state (`@if (count($images) === 0)`) and a bottom CTA linking to `route('admissions')`.

Confirm what changes are intended before editing.

Build stack: Vite 7 + Tailwind v4 via `@tailwindcss/vite`. `vite.config.js` inputs `resources/css/app.css` and `resources/js/app.js`. ### 7. Config / database migrations for gallery images **None exist.** Migrations directory contains only: `create_users_table`, `create_cache_table`, `create_jobs_table`, and `2026_06_30_000000_create_contacts_table`. Models: only `Contact` and `User` — **no `Image` or `Gallery` model**. Config files are all standard Laravel defaults (no gallery config). Gallery images are entirely hardcoded (in the controller for the gallery page, and separately re-hardcoded in `home.blade.php` L369-375 for the homepage preview — note the two lists are not shared/synced). --- ### Cross-codebase search results - **"gallery"** → 11 files: `PageController.php`, `web.php`, `gallery.blade.php`, `home.blade.php`, `navbar.blade.php`, `footer.blade.php`, `PROJECT_DOCUMENTATION.md` (+ compiled view cache files). - **"lightbox"** → 5 files: `PROJECT_DOCUMENTATION.md`, `app.css`, `app.js`, `layouts/app.blade.php`, `pages/gallery.blade.php`. - **"reveal"** → 9 files: `app.css`, `app.js`, `section-header.blade.php`, and pages `about/academics/admissions/contact/gallery/home.blade.php` (the `.reveal` class is used site-wide, not gallery-specific). ### The gallery view itself (`gallery.blade.php`) Extends `layouts.app`, title "Gallery | Abu Aneefah Islamic Academy". Renders a **masonry-style grid** (`columns-1 sm:columns-2 lg:columns-3` with `break-inside-avoid`) looping `$images`. Each item is an `` with `data-lightbox="{{ asset($image['src']) }}"` and `data-alt`, plus the `.reveal` class for scroll animation, hover zoom (`group-hover:scale-110`), and an overlay "eye" icon + caption. Includes an empty-state (`@if (count($images) === 0)`) and a bottom CTA linking to `route('admissions')`. Next steps: If the goal is to enhance the gallery, the natural next steps would be: (a) decide whether to keep images hardcoded or introduce an `images`/`gallery` migration + model so they're DB-driven (currently the homepage preview and gallery page maintain two separate hardcoded lists); (b) note that the lightbox markup lives globally in the layout (`#lightbox`) and JS in `resources/js/app.js` — any lightbox changes affect all pages using `[data-lightbox]`; (c) the lightbox currently lacks prev/next navigation and is not lazy-loaded; (d) the `.reveal` scroll-reveal is site-wide via a shared IntersectionObserver in app.js. ## Gallery Page Context — Full Report The gallery is implemented entirely with a **custom-built lightbox and scroll-reveal** (no third-party lightbox/animation libraries). Images are **hardcoded** in the controller — there is **no database table, model, or migration** for gallery images. --- ### 1. Controller method — `app/Http/Controllers/PageController.php` The `gallery()` method hardcodes an array of 6 images and passes them to the view via `compact('images')`: ```php public function gallery() { $images = [ ['src' => 'images/classroom.jpg', 'alt' => 'Students studying in classroom'], ['src' => 'images/students-girls.jpg', 'alt' => 'Female students in uniform'], ['src' => 'images/students-group.jpg', 'alt' => 'Students with their teacher'], ['src' => 'images/islamic-architecture.jpg', 'alt' => 'Islamic architecture and mosque'], ['src' => 'images/admissions-flyer.jpg', 'alt' => 'Admissions flyer'], ['src' => 'images/admissions-student.jpg', 'alt' => 'Student during admission'], ]; return view('pages.gallery', compact('images')); } ``` Note: `PageController` also has `home()`, `about()`, `admissions()`, `academics()`. Other controllers: `AdminController`, `ContactController`, base `Controller`. ### 2. Route — `routes/web.php` ```php Route::get('/gallery', [PageController::class, 'gallery'])->name('gallery'); ``` The navbar (`resources/views/components/navbar.blade.php` L7) links to it: `['route' => 'gallery', 'label' => 'Gallery']`. The home page's "Gallery Preview" section also links via `route('gallery')`. ### 3. Layout — `resources/views/layouts/app.blade.php` - Asset includes via Vite: `@vite(['resources/css/app.css', 'resources/js/app.js'])` - Google Fonts: Amiri (Arabic) + Plus Jakarta Sans - **No external lightbox library is loaded** (no GLightbox/PhotoSwipe/Litebox/jQuery — none in package.json either) - A **custom lightbox overlay is hardcoded in the layout** (global, not page-specific): ```html
×
@yield('scripts') ``` The layout uses `` and `` Blade components and yields `content`, `title`, and `scripts`. ### 4. JavaScript — `resources/js/` Only two files exist: `app.js` and `bootstrap.js` (bootstrap.js just sets up axios). **No separate gallery/lightbox JS file.** All gallery-related JS is inline in `resources/js/app.js` inside a single `DOMContentLoaded` handler: - **Mobile menu toggle** (`#nav-toggle` / `#nav-menu`) - **Scroll reveal** via `IntersectionObserver`: selects all `.reveal` elements, adds `.revealed` class when intersecting (threshold 0.15, rootMargin `0px 0px -50px 0px`), then unobserves. - **Custom lightbox**: selects `[data-lightbox]` links; on click reads `data-lightbox` (src) + `data-alt` (alt), sets `#lightbox-img`, adds `.active` to `#lightbox`, hides body scroll. Closes on `#lightbox-close` click, overlay click, or `Escape` key. - **Navbar shadow on scroll** (`#navbar`). ### 5. CSS/SCSS — `resources/css/app.css` Only one CSS file (no SCSS/SASS exists). Tailwind v4 (`@import 'tailwindcss';`) with `@source` directives. Custom theme: primary green palette + gold, fonts, and keyframe animations (`fade-in`, `slide-up`, `fade-in-up`). Gallery/lightbox-relevant custom CSS: ```css /* Scroll reveal */ .reveal { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(40px); transition: opacity 0.7s ease-out, transform 0.7s ease-out; } .reveal.revealed { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); } /* Lightbox */ .lightbox-overlay { position: fixed; inset: 0; z-index: 100; display: flex; ... opacity: 0; pointer-events: none; transition: opacity 0.3s ease; } .lightbox-overlay.active { opacity: 1; pointer-events: auto; } .lightbox-overlay img { max-width: 90vw; max-height: 85vh; ... transform: scale(0.9); transition: transform 0.3s ease; } .lightbox-overlay.active img { transform: scale(1); } .lightbox-close { ... } .lightbox-close:hover { color: #86efac; } ``` ### 6. package.json dependencies ```json "devDependencies": { "@tailwindcss/vite": "^4.0.0", "axios": "^1.11.0", "concurrently": "^9.0.1", "laravel-vite-plugin": "^2.0.0", "tailwindcss": "^4.0.0", "vite": "^7.0.7" } ``` No `dependencies` block. **No lightbox, jQuery, AOS, GSAP, or any animation/image library** — everything is custom. Build stack: Vite 7 + Tailwind v4 via `@tailwindcss/vite`. `vite.config.js` inputs `resources/css/app.css` and `resources/js/app.js`. ### 7. Config / database migrations for gallery images **None exist.** Migrations directory contains only: `create_users_table`, `create_cache_table`, `create_jobs_table`, and `2026_06_30_000000_create_contacts_table`. Models: only `Contact` and `User` — **no `Image` or `Gallery` model**. Config files are all standard Laravel defaults (no gallery config). Gallery images are entirely hardcoded (in the controller for the gallery page, and separately re-hardcoded in `home.blade.php` L369-375 for the homepage preview — note the two lists are not shared/synced). --- ### Cross-codebase search results - **"gallery"** → 11 files: `PageController.php`, `web.php`, `gallery.blade.php`, `home.blade.php`, `navbar.blade.php`, `footer.blade.php`, `PROJECT_DOCUMENTATION.md` (+ compiled view cache files). - **"lightbox"** → 5 files: `PROJECT_DOCUMENTATION.md`, `app.css`, `app.js`, `layouts/app.blade.php`, `pages/gallery.blade.php`. - **"reveal"** → 9 files: `app.css`, `app.js`, `section-header.blade.php`, and pages `about/academics/admissions/contact/gallery/home.blade.php` (the `.reveal` class is used site-wide, not gallery-specific). ### The gallery view itself (`gallery.blade.php`) Extends `layouts.app`, title "Gallery | Abu Aneefah Islamic Academy". Renders a **masonry-style grid** (`columns-1 sm:columns-2 lg:columns-3` with `break-inside-avoid`) looping `$images`. Each item is an `` with `data-lightbox="{{ asset($image['src']) }}"` and `data-alt`, plus the `.reveal` class for scroll animation, hover zoom (`group-hover:scale-110`), and an overlay "eye" icon + caption. Includes an empty-state (`@if (count($images) === 0)`) and a bottom CTA linking to `route('admissions')`.
args.next_steps: If the goal is to enhance the gallery, the natural next steps would be: (a) decide whether to keep images hardcoded or introduce an `images`/`gallery` migration + model so they're DB-driven (currently the homepage preview and gallery page maintain two separate hardcoded lists); (b) note that the lightbox markup lives globally in the layout (`#lightbox`) and JS in `resources/js/app.js` — any lightbox changes affect all pages using `[data-lightbox]`; (c) the lightbox currently lacks prev/next navigation and is not lazy-loaded; (d) the `.reveal` scroll-reveal is site-wide via a shared IntersectionObserver in app.js. Confirm what changes are intended before editing.

Gallery/lightbox-relevant custom CSS: ```css /* Scroll reveal */ .reveal { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(40px); transition: opacity 0.7s ease-out, transform 0.7s ease-out; } .reveal.revealed { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); } /* Lightbox */ .lightbox-overlay { position: fixed; inset: 0; z-index: 100; display: flex; ... opacity: 0; pointer-events: none; transition: opacity 0.3s ease; } .lightbox-overlay.active { opacity: 1; pointer-events: auto; } .lightbox-overlay img { max-width: 90vw; max-height: 85vh; ... transform: scale(0.9); transition: transform 0.3s ease; } .lightbox-overlay.active img { transform: scale(1); } .lightbox-close { ... } .lightbox-close:hover { color: #86efac; } ``` ### 6. package.json dependencies ```json "devDependencies": { "@tailwindcss/vite": "^4.0.0", "axios": "^1.11.0", "concurrently": "^9.0.1", "laravel-vite-plugin": "^2.0.0", "tailwindcss": "^4.0.0", "vite": "^7.0.7" } ``` No `dependencies` block. **No lightbox, jQuery, AOS, GSAP, or any animation/image library** — everything is custom. Build stack: Vite 7 + Tailwind v4 via `@tailwindcss/vite`. `vite.config.js` inputs `resources/css/app.css` and `resources/js/app.js`. ### 7. Config / database migrations for gallery images **None exist.** Migrations directory contains only: `create_users_table`, `create_cache_table`, `create_jobs_table`, and `2026_06_30_000000_create_contacts_table`. Models: only `Contact` and `User` — **no `Image` or `Gallery` model**. Config files are all standard Laravel defaults (no gallery config). Gallery images are entirely hardcoded (in the controller for the gallery page, and separately re-hardcoded in `home.blade.php` L369-375 for the homepage preview — note the two lists are not shared/synced). --- ### Cross-codebase search results - **"gallery"** → 11 files: `PageController.php`, `web.php`, `gallery.blade.php`, `home.blade.php`, `navbar.blade.php`, `footer.blade.php`, `PROJECT_DOCUMENTATION.md` (+ compiled view cache files). - **"lightbox"** → 5 files: `PROJECT_DOCUMENTATION.md`, `app.css`, `app.js`, `layouts/app.blade.php`, `pages/gallery.blade.php`. - **"reveal"** → 9 files: `app.css`, `app.js`, `section-header.blade.php`, and pages `about/academics/admissions/contact/gallery/home.blade.php` (the `.reveal` class is used site-wide, not gallery-specific). ### The gallery view itself (`gallery.blade.php`) Extends `layouts.app`, title "Gallery | Abu Aneefah Islamic Academy". Renders a **masonry-style grid** (`columns-1 sm:columns-2 lg:columns-3` with `break-inside-avoid`) looping `$images`. Each item is an `` with `data-lightbox="{{ asset($image['src']) }}"` and `data-alt`, plus the `.reveal` class for scroll animation, hover zoom (`group-hover:scale-110`), and an overlay "eye" icon + caption. Includes an empty-state (`@if (count($images) === 0)`) and a bottom CTA linking to `route('admissions')`. Next steps: If the goal is to enhance the gallery, the natural next steps would be: (a) decide whether to keep images hardcoded or introduce an `images`/`gallery` migration + model so they're DB-driven (currently the homepage preview and gallery page maintain two separate hardcoded lists); (b) note that the lightbox markup lives globally in the layout (`#lightbox`) and JS in `resources/js/app.js` — any lightbox changes affect all pages using `[data-lightbox]`; (c) the lightbox currently lacks prev/next navigation and is not lazy-loaded; (d) the `.reveal` scroll-reveal is site-wide via a shared IntersectionObserver in app.js. Confirm what changes are intended before editing.

functionDeclarations.name: glob
functionDeclarations.description: Efficiently finds files matching specific glob patterns (e.g., `src/**/*.ts`, `**/*.md`), returning absolute paths sorted by modification time (newest first). Ideal for quickly locating files based on their name or path structure, especially in large codebases.

pattern.description: The glob pattern to match against (e.g., '**/*.py', 'docs/*.md').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to the directory to search within. If omitted, searches the root directory.

case_sensitive.description: Optional: Whether the search should be case-sensitive. Defaults to false.

include_hidden.description: Optional: Whether to descend into dot-prefixed directories (e.g. '.git/', '.codely-cli/') and match dot-prefixed files. Defaults to false to mirror search content tools. Set to true to scan hidden paths explicitly.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of file paths to return (default: 100). Use to limit output for broad patterns. When the cap is hit the result is paginated; pass 'offset' set to the previous offset+maxResults to continue.

offset.description: Optional: Number of matching file paths to skip before returning results. Use this to continue after a truncated result's next offset.

functionDeclarations.name: list_directory
functionDeclarations.description: Lists the names of files and subdirectories directly within a specified directory path. Can optionally ignore entries matching provided glob patterns.

path.description: The absolute path to the directory to list (must be absolute, not relative)

ignore.description: List of glob patterns to ignore

functionDeclarations.name: read_file
functionDeclarations.description: Reads and returns the content of a specified file. By default reads up to 2000 lines starting from the beginning of the file. Files whose selected range exceeds 256 KB are truncated with an advisory; use 'offset' and 'limit' only if the file is too large to read at once — it's recommended to read the whole file by not providing these parameters. Handles text files. For images, PDFs, videos, audio or other multimedia files, this tool cannot present their contents to the model — use the 'analyze_multimedia' tool instead.

absolute_path.description: The absolute path to the file to read (e.g., '/home/user/project/file.txt'). Relative paths are not supported. You must provide an absolute path.

offset.description: Optional: For text files, the 0-based line number to start reading from. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once. Note: search_file_content reports matches as 1-based `Lxx` line numbers; to jump from a hit at `L42` here, subtract 1 — pass `offset: 41` (i.e. `offset = lineNumber - 1`).

limit.description: Optional: For text files, the maximum number of lines to read. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

functionDeclarations.name: run_shell_command
functionDeclarations.description:
This tool executes a given shell command using the best available Windows shell (PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt).

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
- Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.

command.description: Exact command to execute using the best available Windows shell (automatically selected from PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt). Quote any file paths containing spaces with double quotes.

description.description: Brief description of the command for the user. Be specific and concise. Ideally a single sentence. Can be up to 3 sentences for clarity. No line breaks.

directory.description: (OPTIONAL) The absolute path of the directory to run the command in. If not provided, the project root directory is used. Must be a directory within the workspace and must already exist.

functionDeclarations.name: search_file_content
functionDeclarations.description: Searches for a regular expression pattern within the content of files in a specified directory, a specific file, or the current working directory. The search backend is ripgrep, so regex patterns use ripgrep/Rust regex syntax, not JavaScript RegExp syntax. If you need to search literal text that contains regex metacharacters (for example $, {, [, (, or \), set fixed_strings: true or -F: true instead of escaping by hand. Can filter files by a glob pattern when searching directories. By default, returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers; set output_mode to "files_with_matches" for just file paths or "count" for per-file match totals. Each matched line is truncated at 500 characters with a "[line truncated from N chars]" suffix to keep minified or base64 content from blowing up context.

pattern.description: The regular expression pattern to search for within file contents. This is interpreted by ripgrep/Rust regex, not JavaScript RegExp; avoid JS-only features such as lookbehind and backreferences. Regex syntax errors are returned by ripgrep. For literal text, especially strings containing regex metacharacters like $, {, [, (, or \, prefer fixed_strings: true or -F: true. When passing JSON args, escape only for JSON once: to match literal ${, use pattern "\\$\\{" (the regex engine sees \$\{), not "\\\\$\\\\{".

path.description: Optional: A path relative to the workspace root, or an absolute path to a directory or file to search within. If a file path is provided, searches only within that file. If a directory path is provided, searches all files in that directory. If omitted, searches the current working directory.

glob.description: Optional: A glob pattern to filter which files are searched (e.g., '*.js', '*.{ts,tsx}', 'src/**'). Single string only — combine multiple patterns via whitespace/comma separation (e.g. '*.js *.ts', '*.js,*.ts') or brace expansion ('*.{ts,tsx}'). Only applicable when searching directories, ignored for file searches. If omitted, searches all files (respecting potential global ignores).

output_mode.description: Optional: Output format. 'content' (default) returns matching lines with line numbers and optional context; 'files_with_matches' returns only the list of matching file paths; 'count' returns per-file match counts.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of matches to return to prevent context overflow (default: 250, max: 1000). Values above 1000 are clamped automatically. Use lower values for broad searches, higher for specific searches. Pass 0 for unlimited.

head_limit.description: Optional: Claude Code aligned alias for 'maxResults'. When both are provided, head_limit wins. Pass 0 for unlimited (use sparingly — large result sets waste context).

offset.description: Optional: Number of matches to skip before returning results. Use this to continue after a truncated result's next offset.

-A.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -A.

-B.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -B.

-C.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before and after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -C.

context.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before and after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for -C.

-n.description: Optional: Whether to include line numbers in 'content' output. Defaults to true; set to false to omit them.

-i.description: Optional: Case-insensitive matching. Defaults to false (case-sensitive), matching ripgrep behavior.

fixed_strings.description: Optional: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression. Alias for -F.

-F.description: Optional: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression. Alias for fixed_strings.

type.description: Optional: ripgrep file type filter (rg --type). Common types: js, py, rust, go, java, etc. Accepts any alias from `rg --type-list`; the value is forwarded verbatim to ripgrep.

multiline.description: Optional: Enable multiline matching where patterns may span newlines (passes -U and --multiline-dotall to ripgrep). Only meaningful for 'content' output_mode. ^/$ anchors keep line boundaries behavior for portability across search backends.

include_hidden.description: Optional: Whether to descend into dot-prefixed directories (e.g. '.git/', '.codely-cli/') and match dot-prefixed files. Defaults to false to mirror ripgrep's default hidden-skip behaviour. Set to true to scan hidden paths explicitly (passes --hidden to ripgrep).

## Gallery Page Context — Full Report

The gallery is implemented entirely with a **custom-built lightbox and scroll-reveal** (no third-party lightbox/animation libraries). Images are **hardcoded** in the controller — there is **no database table, model, or migration** for gallery images.

---

### 1. Controller method — `app/Http/Controllers/PageController.php`
The `gallery()` method hardcodes an array of 6 images and passes them to the view via `compact('images')`:
```php
public function gallery()
{
    $images = [
        ['src' => 'images/classroom.jpg', 'alt' => 'Students studying in classroom'],
        ['src' => 'images/students-girls.jpg', 'alt' => 'Female students in uniform'],
        ['src' => 'images/students-group.jpg', 'alt' => 'Students with their teacher'],
        ['src' => 'images/islamic-architecture.jpg', 'alt' => 'Islamic architecture and mosque'],
        ['src' => 'images/admissions-flyer.jpg', 'alt' => 'Admissions flyer'],
        ['src' => 'images/admissions-student.jpg', 'alt' => 'Student during admission'],
    ];
    return view('pages.gallery', compact('images'));
}
```
Note: `PageController` also has `home()`, `about()`, `admissions()`, `academics()`. Other controllers: `AdminController`, `ContactController`, base `Controller`.

### 2. Route — `routes/web.php`
```php
Route::get('/gallery', [PageController::class, 'gallery'])->name('gallery');
```
The navbar (`resources/views/components/navbar.blade.php` L7) links to it: `['route' => 'gallery', 'label' => 'Gallery']`. The home page's "Gallery Preview" section also links via `route('gallery')`.

### 3. Layout — `resources/views/layouts/app.blade.php`
- Asset includes via Vite: `@vite(['resources/css/app.css', 'resources/js/app.js'])`
- Google Fonts: Amiri (Arabic) + Plus Jakarta Sans
- **No external lightbox library is loaded** (no GLightbox/PhotoSwipe/Litebox/jQuery — none in package.json either)
- A **custom lightbox overlay is hardcoded in the layout** (global, not page-specific):
